content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves gently to the left ahead with the car well-positioned near center, on course with the curve visible in the distance.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 60, target = 60 + 5 = 65
- Waypoint 65: (3.4036, 4.2174)
- Car position: (4.1329, 3.5094)
- Bearing = atan2(4.2174 - 3.5094, 3.4036 - 4.1329) = atan2(0.7080, -0.7293)
- = 180 - atan2(0.7080, 0.7293) = 180 - 44.16° = 135.84°
- Current heading: 141.70°
- Delta = 135.84 - 141.70 = -5.86° → slight right correction needed

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves left ahead but waypoint 65 is slightly to the right of current heading (delta=-5.86°), so a small right correction of -5° is appropriate to stay on the waypoint line.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track has a left-curving section around waypoints 55-70, heading southwest",
    "Car is consistently slightly right of center (~0.12m) but within safe margin (track width 0.76m)",
    "Full circuit ~17.7m, speed 1.5 is max and appropriate for moderate curves",
    "Use steering_angle=0 for |delta| <5° to avoid oscillation on near-straight sections",
    "Delta near +5-9° means gentle left correction — use steering_angle=5",
    "Delta near -5-9° means gentle right correction — use steering_angle=-5",
    "Car is maintaining 1.5 speed well through this section",
    "Waypoints 60-70 show continuing left curve (south-southwest)",
    "5° steering is appropriate for delta 5-10° range to avoid overshoot",
    "When delta drops below 5°, immediately switch to 0 steering to prevent oscillation"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [4.375, 4.296, 4.207, 4.133],
    "y": [3.3255, 3.3848, 3.4532, 3.5094],
    "yaw": [140.394, 141.148, 141.464, 141.698]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": -5.86,
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": -5.0
}
```
